records.title: Micron’s Blowout Is About to Test the Summer Fade the Rally Crowd
records.claim: Micron delivered a blowout earnings report with revenue of $41.46B (vs. $36B expected) and guided Q4 revenue to ~$50B (vs. $43B Street estimate). This demonstrates that AI demand continues to outrun memory supply, giving producers strong pricing power. However, investors should note that betting against Micron now requires believing that shortages, hyperscaler spending, and customer urgency all cool simultaneously.

records.title: AMD vs. Marvell Technology: Which Will Be the Next Trillion-Dollar Stock?

However, both companies are well-positioned to benefit from the AI boom, particularly as demand for custom ASICs and CPUs increases with the shift toward agentic AI.

records.title: Could SpaceX Stock Make You a Millionaire? This Is the Simple Answer

records.title: Better Buy: SpaceX or the "Magnificent Seven"?
records.claim: SpaceX's recent IPO has brought it to trillion-dollar valuation comparable to tech giants, but the article argues it remains overvalued compared to the Magnificent Seven. The author recommends the Magnificent Seven companies as better investments with less speculative valuation.

records.title: Danaher Is Down 22% This Year. Is the Masimo Acquisition the Entry Point Investors Have Been Waiting for?

The company is seeing 30% year-over-year growth in equipment orders, benefiting from a resurgence in life sciences M&A activity. Trading at 20x forward earnings—below its historical average—the stock may represent a buying opportunity if management successfully integrates Masimo.

records.title: This Artificial Intelligence (AI) Chip Stock Is Dominating the Inference Era. It Could Be the Biggest Winner of This Megatrend (Hint: It's Not AMD or Broadcom)

records.title: Better Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock to Buy: SpaceX vs. Nvidia
records.claim: The article compares SpaceX and Nvidia as AI investment options. records.title: The Chip Sell-Off Is Not Just About AI: There’s a Bigger Problem Under the Hood
records.claim: Tuesday's semiconductor sell-off extends beyond AI concerns to reveal structural market issues. Leveraged ETFs like SOXL use daily rebalancing that amplifies downturns, forcing selling that exacerbates price declines. South Korea's regulatory warning about overheated leveraged chip ETFs triggered a 10% Kospi drop, affecting global memory chip stocks. The sector faces a critical test with Micron's earnings on June 24, where forward guidance on HBM demand and data center orders will determine if the AI spending cycle remains intact.

records.title: SpaceX Valuation Loses Altitude as AI Risks Mount
records.claim: SpaceX's stock has fallen 25% from its $225 IPO peak to $165, as investors reassess the company's strategic pivot toward AI data centers and software acquisitions. The $60 billion all-stock acquisition of Anysphere, combined with a $6.3 billion computing power agreement with Reflection AI, is straining cash flow and diluting shareholder value. Additionally, Starlink's average revenue per user has declined from $99 to $66 monthly, while the company faces potential merger rumors with Tesla. Rising interest rates and a microscopic 5% public float are amplifying the sell-off.

records.title: This Nvidia-Backed Company Just Entered the $154 Billion AI Photonics Market. Does This Make the $13 Stock a No-Brainer Buy?
records.claim: Nokia is expanding its role in AI infrastructure through silicon photonics and optical networking solutions. The company announced expansion of photonic chip operations in Pennsylvania and reported strong Q1 2026 results with 49% YoY growth in its AI & Cloud segment and $1.1 billion in new AI infrastructure orders. Goldman Sachs projects the optical networking market tied to AI will grow from $15 billion in 2026 to $154 billion by 2028, with co-packaged optics representing a $91 billion opportunity. Recent insider buying by Nokia executives, combined with Nvidia's $1 billion strategic investment, suggests the stock has further upside potential.

records.title: SpaceX Lockup Expiration: Will Insider Selling Sink the Stock?
records.claim: SpaceX is using a staggered lockup expiration schedule to manage insider selling over the next year, rather than a single cliff date. While this approach spreads out potential downward pressure, the stock faces headwinds from high valuations relative to fundamentals and post-IPO hype fading. The biggest expiration on June 14, 2027 will unlock 6.4 billion shares including those held by CEO Elon Musk.

records.title: Alphabet's Google Cloud Backlog Just Hit a Record $462 Billion. Time to Buy the Stock?
records.claim: Alphabet's Google Cloud division has reached a record $462 billion backlog with 63% year-over-year revenue growth, outpacing competitors AWS (28%) and Azure (40%). The company is investing heavily in data center infrastructure, with 2026 capex guidance of $180-190 billion and plans for even higher spending in 2027. This positions Alphabet for continued growth and a transformation from an advertising-focused to a more balanced cloud-driven business.

records.title: Right After Becoming a Trillionaire, Elon Musk "Sold" 17.5 Million Tesla Shares. Here's Why This Isn't a Big Deal.
records.claim: Elon Musk exercised 304 million Tesla stock options granted in 2018, using net share settlement to pay the exercise price by withholding 17.5 million shares. Rather than selling, this transaction actually increased Musk's ownership stake to 19.9% and added 286.4 million restricted shares to his position, demonstrating alignment with Tesla shareholders.

records.title: Google Tests Major Support as AI Spending Fears Return
